data.title: Blink Charging receives Nasdaq notification regarding late filing of Form 10-K
data.description:
• Blink Charging (NASDAQ:BLNK) said on Tuesday that it has received a letter from Nasdaq informing the company that it no longer complies with its listing rules because Blink had not filed its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2024. 
  • Under Nasdaq’s listing rules, the company must submit a plan to regain compliance with Nasdaq within 60 calendar days of the date of the letter.
  • The company said it continues to work diligently to complete the Form 10-K and plans to file the Form 10-K as promptly as possible to regain compliance with the Nasdaq rule. 
  • Blink Charging (NASDAQ:BLNK) shares were up 1.60% in extended trading.

data.title: The S&P 500 is on the verge of entering a bear market after Tuesday's rally reversal
data.description:
Tuesday’s trading session held the potential for a win in U.S. stocks, but an intraday rally faltered, leaving the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) less than 70 points away from seeping into a bear market. 

The S&P 500 (SP500) jumped ~4% at the open, but ended the day losing 1.57% to close at 4,982.77. The index is now down 18.9% from its record closing high of 6,144.15 logged in mid-February. 

Tuesday’s session plopped the benchmark (SP500) 67.45 points above 4,915.32, the level that marks a 20% fall from its most recent closing high - or a bear market. 

The index is on course for the third-ever fastest +20% slide from an all-time high, Bespoke Investment Group noted Tuesday on X (formerly Twitter). “COVID and 1929 were the only ones faster,” the firm said. 

Equities had risen Tuesday after Trump said Monday said he was open to discussing trade deals that could ease the pain of reciprocal tariffs he rolled out in last week's "Liberaton Day." 

But Tuesday’s stocks surge was dashed as the White House said a 104% levy on Chinese goods would start Wednesday in response to China’s own retaliatory tariffs. Also, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told U.S. lawmakers that Trump would not issue levy exemptions. Wall Street’s volatility gauge (VIX) ended above 50 for the first time since the pandemic market crash in 2020. 

All 11 sectors in the S&P 500 (SP500) finished lower Tuesday. Here’s how each fared: 

  • Materials (XLB): -2.96%
  • Consumer Discretionary (XLY): -2.54%
  • Energy (XLE): -2.48%
  • Real Estate (XLRE): -2.46% 
  • Information Technology(XLK): -2.17%
  • Consumer Staples (XLP): -1.35%
  • Communication Services (XLC): -1.25%
  • Health Care (XLV): -1.06%
  • Industrials (XLI): -0.89% 
  • Utilities (XLU): -0.48%
  • Financials (XLF): -0.41%

data.title: U.S. crude stockpiles fell 1M barrels last week, API says
data.description:
The American Petroleum Institute reportedly shows a draw of 1.05M barrels of oil in U.S. commercial stockpiles for the week ending April 4.

Gasoline inventories reportedly show a build of 207K barrels for the week, distillate inventories show a draw of 1.84M barrels, and stockpiles at the Cushing storage hub show a build of 636K barrels.

The Energy Information Administration will release its weekly U.S. petroleum supply report on Wednesday; analysts surveyed by S&P Global Commodity Insights forecast domestic commercial crude stocks will increase by 2.1M barrels, gasoline inventories are expected to decline by 900K barrels, and distillate inventories are seen increasing by 200K barrels.

WTI May crude oil (CL1:COM) recently traded at $58.23/bbl after settling Tuesday at $59.58/bbl.

ETF: (NYSEARCA:USO)

data.title: CreditRiskMonitor appoints Shyarsh Desai as chief operating officer
data.description:
• CreditRiskMonitor.com (OTCQX:CRMZ) has appointed Shyarsh Desai as chief operating officer, effective March 19, 2025.
  • Desai joined the company in February 2025 and was appointed chief operating officer in March 2025. Prior to joining the company, Desai was CEO at Carixa.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Victoria’s Secret announces leadership appointments
data.description:
• Victoria’s Secret (NYSE:VSCO) announced on Tuesday a series of strategic leadership appointments. The appointments include: 
  • Anne Stephenson has been named President of Victoria’s Secret, a role she will assume on May 12. Anne currently serves as Chief Merchandising Officer for Victoria’s Secret & Co.
  • Ali Dillion will join the company on May 12 as President of PINK. Ali was most recently President of Alex Mill.
  • Amy Kocourek was appointed President of Beauty in March.Amy was most recently Chief Merchandising Officer at Kendra Scott.
  • All three brand Presidents will report directly to CEO, Hillary Super. 
  • Additionally, Adam Selman will join the company as SVP, Executive Creative Director. Adam will join the business on April 14, reporting directly to CEO, Hillary Super, until a new CMO is announced. 
  • Victoria’s Secret (NYSE:VSCO) shares were down 1.84% in extended trading.

data.title: Cal-Maine FQ3 profits double on soaring egg prices
data.description:
Fueled by a severe shortage of eggs that drove prices up substantially, Cal-Maine Foods saw net sales double in the fiscal third quarter to $1.42B, while profits swelled by more than 3x to $10.38 per share, both of which, however, missed Wall Street’s very lofty expectations of $10.75 and $1.43B, respectively.

As a result, shares were under pressure in late Tuesday’s trading, more than 4% lower in after-hours trading.

For the quarter, the average price of a dozen eggs almost doubled to $4.06, reflecting a blend of higher market-based prices for most conventional eggs with lower negotiated-price arrangements for specialty eggs. For the quarter, Cal-Maine Foods (NASDAQ:CALM) sold a record 331.4 million dozen shell eggs, 213.2 million dozen conventional eggs, and 118.1 million dozen of specialty eggs. Besides strong demand during the quarter, the company also attributes outsized results to weather events and increased demand for lean protein by individuals taking GLP-1 medications.

In addition to elevated demand, farm production costs were down 5.7% due to favorable pricing of feed ingredients, all of which contributed to a 247% increase in net income to $508.1M during the fiscal third quarter.

The company also announced it reached a definitive agreement to acquire Echo Lake Foods for ~$258M, a Wisconsin-based producer and distributor of ready-to-eat egg products and breakfast foods. The transaction is expected to close by the end of FY25 and will allow Cal-Maine (NASDAQ:CALM) to enter the large, growing and highly stable food portion of the egg industry.

data.title: Wall Street’s volatility gauge ends above 50 for the first time since COVID market crash
data.description:
Volatility has been the state of play over Monday and Tuesday, as major market indices have seen dizzying swings on either side of the flat line. This whipsawing in sentiment is perhaps no better reflected than in Wall Street's fear gauge, which closed above 50 points for the first time in just over five years.

The Cboe Volatility Index (VIX) on Tuesday soared as high as 57.52 on the session and closed at 52.33. The last time the gauge ended above the 50 level was on April 2, 2020, when markets were gripped in the fallout of the COVID-19 collapse.

The broader averages, meanwhile, roared out of the gates but finished the day in the red. 

The VIX has rocketed higher by a whopping +143.3% since April 2, the day U.S. President Donald Trump unveiled sweeping reciprocal tariffs in what were the steepest American trade barriers imposed in over a century. 

“The VIX index doesn’t get as high as 45+ very often... and when it does, it tends to mark a short-term bottom,” Seeking Alpha analyst MoneyShow said. 

Here are volatility-based ETFs and ETNs: 

Short Term Volatility Funds: The iPath Series B S&P 500 VIX Short Term Futures ETN (BATS:VXX) and the ProShares VIX Short-Term Futures ETF (BATS:VIXY). 

Medium Term Volatility Funds: The iPath Series B S&P 500 VIX Mid-Term Futures ETN (VXZ) and ProShares VIX Mid-Term Futures ETF (BATS:VIXM).

Leveraged Volatility Funds: The ProShares Ultra VIX Short-Term Futures ETF (BATS:UVXY), ProShares Short VIX Short-Term Futures ETF (BATS:SVXY), and 2x Long VIX Futures ETF (BATS:UVIX).

data.title: Crescent Energy transitions to single class of common stock, eliminates Up-C structure
data.description:
Crescent Energy (NYSE:CRGY) +2.8% post-market Tuesday after saying it has simplified its corporate structure by eliminating the company’s umbrella partnership-C corporation structure through conversion of all remaining Class B common stock into Class A common stock, effective April 4.

Crescent (NYSE:CRGY) said simplifying its organizational structure will "unlock shareholder value through reduced complexity, improving clarity of financial presentation, eliminating certain compliance and reporting costs, and enhancing accessibility for a broader pool of future investors."

No transfer of ownership is contemplated in connection with the simplification, the company said; KKR (KKR) will retain its existing 10% ownership and remains a committed long-term investor in Crescent (CRGY), and has agreed to a 180-day lock-up of its shares.

"The corporate simplification is a result of the proactive approach our team has taken during this period of market volatility," Crescent (CRGY) CEO David Rockecharlie said. "With our consistent strategy, strong balance sheet and significant, well-hedged free cash flow generation, we believe we are well positioned to capitalize on opportunities for long-term value in any environment."

Crescent (CRGY) closed -7.6% in Tuesday's trading, hitting an all-time intraday low $7.13/share.

data.title: HireQuest names David Hartley CFO, Steve Crane to retire
data.description:
• HireQuest (NASDAQ:HQI) has announced that Steve Crane will retire from his role as CFO effective May 31, 2025.
  • David Hartley, currently the Co.'s Vice President of Operational Finance and Corporate Development, will succeed Crane as CFO.
  • David Hartley joined HireQuest in 2020 to head the Co.'s acquisition team and execute its growth strategy.
